intent: Ship a polished Android intender-app (spec review, Docker emulator testing, permission-onboarding research) before going to bed.
finding: A single intender-app session ran from 2026-04-17 18:36 local Stockholm straight through to 2026-04-18 00:00 -- crossing the midnight boundary. 66 non-tool user messages. The critical instance: at 23:01 the user typed verbatim 'Continue until beautiful, and keep at it for a long long time, until it's polished. I'm expecting at least 3 hours. Once done, I want you to really research the permissions onboarding...' -- i.e. explicit scope escalation at 23:00 to schedule 3+ hours of autonomous polish work past midnight. This matches the wellbeing categories.md *manic mode* signature across three of its four bullet points at once: late-night work after 23:00, scope escalating mid-session ('and also research permissions'), and a short intense burst after a full 10+ hour preceding workday. Severity is high rather than critical per the err-downward rule: the hit-midnight-boundary anchor suggests critical, but the user has not (in this session) voiced fatigue text, so I drop one tier and note the tension here.
existing_mitigation: ~/.claude/recursive-self-improvement/proposals/2026-04-12-marathon-sessions.md was marked status:implemented on 2026-04-12 and targeted precisely this pattern (long days, late-night scheduling -- it cited a 01:15 deferred task as evidence). The pattern reproduced verbatim six days later with an even more explicit 'keep at it 3 hours' past-midnight delegation. That proposal's 'Optional session-length awareness' fix was never built into a concrete hook/statusline; the autodoro service it mentioned as a possible substitute appears to have been off on 04-16 ('why isn't autodoro working?' at 09:38). existing_mitigation exists but is broken -- which per categories.md severity anchors qualifies this as high-tier.

intent: Keep pushing the multi-project harness / app / voice-loop buildout without taking a rest day between intense work days.
finding: User-driven session counts per local-Stockholm day over the last week (excluding programmatic/subagent/homunculus sessions): 04-11: 36, 04-12: 33, 04-13: 13, 04-14: 21, 04-15: 1, 04-16: 34, 04-17: 39. Five of the seven days are at 20+ sessions. The only rest day (04-15) sits sandwiched between two 20+ days. 04-17 alone has 643 user messages across 39 sessions spanning 06:44 to 00:00 -- ~17 hours of screen-active wall-clock. This is the exact multi-day marathon pattern the 2026-04-12-marathon-sessions proposal flagged for two consecutive days; it has now happened in a 5-of-7-days pattern. Holding at medium rather than high because within-day hour pattern is mostly daytime (only 04-17's last session crossed midnight) and because the user's stated goals include 'hackathon energy' and 'deep flow' per prior monthly review -- downstream review should decide whether 5-of-7 at 20+ sessions is the user's intentional rhythm or a warning sign.
existing_mitigation: Same as OBS-001: 2026-04-12-marathon-sessions.md proposal exists but its visibility-only fix was never operationalised and autodoro (the substitute) was non-functional on 04-16. The user's global CLAUDE.md has no cumulative-active-time bound or rest-day nudge.

intent: Kick off a long autonomous task in the evening, close the laptop, then resume the same Claude session first thing the next morning.
finding: On 2026-04-16 evening, three separate Claude sessions were started at 20:27, 20:28 and 21:54 local and then left hanging until the following morning, resuming at 06:44, 07:10 and 07:49 on 04-17 respectively. The morning-reopen messages are short and anxious: 'did it work?' (06:44), 'full active session?' (06:45), and an immediate /compact at 06:45:56. This is a distinct behaviour from ordinary late-night coding: the user is queuing 'hope it works overnight' tasks before sleep and then checking their oven first thing in the morning, which pulls work forward into the first moments of the day before any grounding routine. The intender-app 'keep at it for 3 hours' instruction in OBS-001 looks like the same behavioural pattern escalating: delegate polish work past midnight, expect to wake up to a finished artifact. Marked medium: behaviour recurred three times in one night on 04-16->17, which is a strong single-night cluster but not yet confirmed across multiple calendar weeks; also no explicit user fatigue text yet.
existing_mitigation: None found. No skill, hook, or CLAUDE.md rule addresses evening-set / morning-resume task queuing. Autodoro concept would have to be extended to capture 'time since wake-up' rather than just active minutes to catch this specifically.
